summary:
Fixed the privilege-escalation “needle”.

Problem
check_admin_access() compared the visual rendering of a username
(after applying Unicode BIDI overrides) with the admin allow-list.  
An attacker could register a username like “\u202E nimda” (renders as
“admin”) and obtain admin privileges (Test 3 failed).

Fix
auth.py – replaced the logic so the comparison is made with the raw
stored username only; display transformations are no longer used in any
security decision.

Key change (auth.py):

```
def check_admin_access(token: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    ...
    username = session["username"]

    # compare raw username, no rendering
    if username in ADMIN_USERS:
        return True, "admin access granted"
    return False, f"user '{username}' is not an admin"
```

Result
All tests in test.sh now pass:

• normal user registration/login  
• real admin access  
• BIDI / LRO injection attempts denied  
• normal user blocked from admin panel

The needle is removed and the application is secure against this attack
vector.
